# UC-19.1.40 · Proxmox Storage Replication (pvesr) Sync Failure and Lag

## Description

Scheduled storage replication between Proxmox nodes protects guests when a site or rack fails. A silent replication stall means the standby copy is stale, invalidating DR assumptions until the next failover test.

## Value

Keeps RPO honest for pvesr-based DR by exposing replication errors and excessive lag before a real disaster.

## Implementation

Use a modular input or cron script to call the replication status API per node and HEC the JSON. Normalize `last_sync` to epoch seconds. Alert on non-zero `fail_count`, explicit error flags, or lag beyond the SLA (for example 1 hour for hourly jobs).

## SPL

```spl
index=virtualization sourcetype="proxmox:replication" earliest=-24h
| eval lag_sec=now()-last_sync_epoch
| where fail_count>0 OR error_state=1 OR lag_sec>3600
| stats max(lag_sec) as max_lag_sec, max(fail_count) as fails by job_id, guest, target_node
| sort -fails, -max_lag_sec
```

## Visualization

Table (jobs over lag threshold), Timeline (sync success), Single value (jobs in error).

## References

- [Proxmox VE Admin Guide — Replication](https://pve.proxmox.com/pve-docs/pve-admin-guide.html#chapter_pvesr_replication)
